groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content: Prices range from $105 to $$22035$per room per month

The furniture is very$22036$
Delivers in 1-2 days
Special offer:
free$22037$with every living room set
Offers cheapest prices for renting furniture and$22040$items

Must have own$22041$
Minimum contract length: six months
See the$22043$for the most up-to-date prices

questions.answer: 239 / two hundred thirty-nine / two hundred and thirty-nine
questions.analysis: 同学你好，这道题的正确答案是239（或two hundred thirty-nine / two hundred and thirty-nine）。我们来看一下为什么是这个答案。题目问的是Peak Rentals公司每月每间房的价格范围上限，表格里已经给出了下限是$105。在听力中，当男士提到“Peak Rentals”这个公司名（这就是我们要找的定位词）后，接着就说到了价格：“the monthly price per room starts at a hundred and five dollars and goes up to two hundred and thirty-nine dollars”。这里的“starts at a hundred and five dollars”（对应题目中的$105）就是信号词，告诉我们价格范围的开始，而“goes up to”这个短语明确表示“上升到……”，后面紧跟的“two hundred and thirty-nine dollars”就是价格范围的上限，也就是题目中需要我们填写的内容。所以，根据听力原文，这里应该填239。

这道题需要你找到描述Peak Rentals公司家具特点的形容词。首先，通过定位词“Peak Rentals”和“furniture”，你能在听力中快速锁定相关内容。当听到男士回答Shelley关于“positive feedback”（积极反馈）时，紧接着提到“the furniture from Peak Rentals is more modern than any of the other companies”，这里明确用“more modern”描述了家具的特点。题目中“the furniture is very【2】”需要一个形容词，“more modern”中的核心形容词是“modern”，且“very modern”语法和语义均符合要求，所以答案是“modern”。

同学你看，这道题问的是Peak Rentals公司的特别优惠：租客厅套装会免费得到什么。首先，我们在题目中找到“Special offer”和“free”这两个定位词。在听力原文中，当男士提到“there’s a special offer at the moment”时，这就是“Special offer”的直接对应，提示我们优惠信息要来了。接着，他说“you’ll also get a lamp at no extra cost”，这里的“at no extra cost”就是“free”的同义替换，非常明确地告诉我们免费得到的东西是“lamp”。虽然前面提到了“chairs”和“TV table”，但那是客厅套装包含的租赁物品，不是免费赠送的，所以答案肯定是“lamp”。

题目表格中需要填写“and Oliver”前的公司名称部分，所以“and Oliver”是定位词。在听力中，当Shelley觉得第一家公司价格过高时，男士提到“Then you could try Aaron and Oliver”，直接出现了定位词“and Oliver”，说明这是新的公司名称。Shelley没听清，追问“what and Oliver”，男士进一步明确回答“Aaron”并拼写“Double-A-R-O-N”，确认了“and Oliver”前的单词是“Aaron”。因此答案是Aaron，注意只需填写一个词，且拼写需准确（双写A）。

这道题需要你找出“Aaron and Oliver”公司收取12%月费的原因。首先，通过定位词“Aaron and Oliver”和“12% monthly fee”，你能在听力中快速找到对应的信息段落。原文提到“they charge an extra twelve per cent every month in case of damage”，这里“twelve per cent every month”是“12% monthly fee”的同义替换，而“in case of”表示“以防……情况”，直接引出了收取费用的原因——“damage”（损坏）。题目中“12% monthly fee for【5】”的“for”正是询问费用的用途，所以“damage”就是正确答案。

同学你好，这道题的正确答案是“electronic”。我们一起来看一下为什么。首先，我们根据表格中的公司名“Larch Furniture”定位到听力中相关的介绍部分。当听到这家公司“has the lowest prices in town”时，这里的“lowest prices”就是题目中“cheapest prices”的同义替换，说明我们找对了信息区域。题目问的是这家公司提供最便宜价格的除了“furniture”（家具）还有什么“items”（物品）。听力中紧接着说“That’s for both furniture and also electronic equipment.”，这里的“and also”对应了题目中的“and”，表明了并列关系。“equipment”是名词，意为“设备”，而题目空格后是“items”（物品），需要一个形容词来修饰“items”，“electronic”（电子的）是形容词，正好可以修饰“items”，表示“电子物品”。而“equipment”本身是名词，不能直接修饰“items”，且题目要求“ONE WORD”，所以“electronic”是正确答案。

同学你看，这道题问的是Larch Furniture公司要求客户必须自己有什么。我们首先通过表格中的公司名“Larch Furniture”定位到听力中介绍这家公司的部分。当听到男士说“There are two things you need to know about Larch Furniture. First of all...”时，就要注意了，这“First of all”后面跟着的就是第一个重要信息。他说“you have to take out insurance on the furniture, and you need to organise that yourself.”，这里的“you have to take out”（你必须办理）和“you need to organise that yourself”（你需要自己安排）正好对应了题目中的“Must have own”（必须有自己的）。所以，这里的“insurance”就是题目要求填写的答案，即客户必须自己办理家具保险。

题目要求填写表格中“Name of company”列的公司名称，定位词是“Rentals”，因为表格中已给出“Rentals”，需要找其前面的单词。听力中，当男士提到“another furniture rental company”（另一家家具租赁公司）时，这是明显的信号词，提示即将介绍新公司。随后直接说明“It’s called Space Rentals”，明确公司名称为“Space Rentals”，因此“Rentals”前的单词“Space”就是正确答案。

这道题问的是关于“Space Rentals”这家公司，去哪里看最新的价格。首先，我们通过定位词“Space Rentals”找到听力中对应的段落。当听到这家公司的名字后，就要留意和“价格”相关的信息。题目中的“most up-to-date prices”（最新价格）在原文中被同义替换成了“their charges”（他们的收费）和“what it would cost you”（要花多少钱）。接着，原文明确说“it’s best to use their app to find out...”，意思是最好用他们的“app”来查询价格。所以，“app”就是正确答案，它直接回答了“See the ____ for the most up-to-date prices”这个问题，即看“app”来获取最新价格。

题目问的是在“delivery”（配送）后“7 days”（7天）内允许做什么。我们首先通过定位词“7 days”和“delivery”找到听力中对应的同义替换“a week”和“receiving it”。在包含这些信号词的句子中，明确提到“you can request exchanges”（你可以要求更换），这里的“can request”（可以要求）就对应了题目中的“are allowed”（被允许）。因此，在7天内被允许的事情就是“exchanges”（更换），所以答案是exchanges。

transcript.en: Part 1
MAN: Good morning.
transcript.cn: 男人：早上好。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Hi. This is Shelley Mayer.
transcript.cn: 谢莉：你好，我是谢莉・梅耶。

transcript.en: I'm renting your house on Archwood Avenue. I'm due to move in next week.
transcript.cn: 我要把你在亚齐伍德大道上的房子租出去。我下周就要搬进去了。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh yes, hello Ms Mayer. What can I do for you?
transcript.cn: 男人：哦，是的，您好，梅耶女士。我能为您做些什么？

transcript.en: SHELLEY: When I viewed the house, I told you I'll most probably need to rent some furniture – at least until I know whether my temporary work contract is going to be made permanent.
transcript.cn: 谢利：当我看房子的时候，我告诉过你我可能需要租一些家具 —— 至少在我确定我的临时工作合同是否会变成永久合同之前。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes of course, I remember. And I said I could give you some information about furniture rental companies in the city.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的，我当然记得。我说过我可以给你提供一些关于城里家具租赁公司的信息。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: That's right.
transcript.cn: 谢利：没错。

transcript.en: MAN: Well, the biggest company is called Peak Rentals.
transcript.cn: 男人：嗯，最大的公司叫峰值租赁公司。

transcript.en: I've recommended them to other people and have always heard positive reports about them.
transcript.cn: 我一直向其他人推荐他们，也总是听到关于他们的积极评价。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Could you give me an idea of their costs?
transcript.cn: 谢利：你能告诉我他们的成本是多少吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Sure, I actually have one of their brochures here.
transcript.cn: 男人：当然，我这里其实有一本他们的小册子。

transcript.en: It says the monthly price per room starts at a hundred and five dollars and goes up to two hundred and thirty-nine dollars – that depends on which rooms you need furniture for, of course.
transcript.cn: 上面说每间房间的月租金从 105 美元起，最高可达 239 美元 —— 当然，这取决于你需要哪些房间的家具。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Sure. It's just to get a general idea of how much it's gonna cost.
transcript.cn: 谢利：当然。只是想大致了解一下要花多少钱。

transcript.en: And you said you had some positive feedback about this company.
transcript.cn: 你说你对这家公司有一些积极的反馈。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, people have mentioned that the furniture from Peak Rentals is more modern than any of the other companies.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的，人们已经提到，Peak Rentals 的家具比其他任何公司都更现代化。

transcript.en: And also, once you place an order, the furniture will be delivered to you in just one or two days.
transcript.cn: 而且，一旦你下了订单，家具就会在一两天内送到你手中。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: That would be really helpful.
transcript.cn: 谢利：那真的会很有帮助。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh, and the brochure says that there's a special offer at the moment.
transcript.cn: 男人：哦，小册子上说现在有特别优惠。

transcript.en: If you rent living room furniture – I believe that's a set of chairs and a TV table, you'll also get a lamp at no extra cost.
transcript.cn: 如果你租用客厅家具 —— 我记得是一套椅子和一张电视桌，你还可以免费获得一盏台灯。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: OK. But, you know, that price range you gave is more than I was hoping to pay.
transcript.cn: 谢利：好的。但是，你知道吗，你给的价格范围比我希望支付的要高。

transcript.en: MAN: Then you could try Aaron and Oliver.
transcript.cn: 男人：那你可以试试亚伦和奥利弗。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Sorry, what and Oliver?
transcript.cn: 谢利：抱歉，那奥利弗呢？

transcript.en: MAN: Aaron. Double-A-R-O-N.
transcript.cn: 男人：亚伦。双 A-R-O-N。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: OK. Are they cheaper?
transcript.cn: 谢利：好的。他们更便宜吗？

transcript.en: MAN: I'd say they're a mid-price company.
transcript.cn: 男人：我会说他们是一家中等价格的公司。

transcript.en: But if you chose them, you need to be aware that they charge an extra twelve per cent every month in case of damage.
transcript.cn: 但如果你选择他们，你需要知道，他们每月会额外收取 12% 的费用，以防损坏。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Oh I see. I'd have to do the math carefully then.
transcript.cn: 谢利：哦，我明白了。那我得仔细算算了。

transcript.en: MAN: Right. But one helpful thing is that they also do cleaning for customers.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的。但有一件有用的事是，他们也为客户做清洁。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: For the furniture?
transcript.cn: 谢利：家具呢？

transcript.en: MAN: For the house.
transcript.cn: 男人：为了房子。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Oh I see. I probably won't need that.
transcript.cn: 谢利：哦，我明白了。我可能不需要那个。

transcript.en: MAN: There's another company called Larch Furniture.
transcript.cn: 男人：还有另一家公司叫拉奇家具。

transcript.en: It's quite new, and it has the lowest prices in town.
transcript.cn: 它相当新，而且价格是城里最低的。

transcript.en: That's for both furniture and also electronic equipment.
transcript.cn: 这包括家具和电子设备。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Well that would be good.
transcript.cn: 谢利：那样就好了。

transcript.en: I'm not bringing much with me, and I won't have much time to go shopping after I start my job.
transcript.cn: 我不会带太多东西，而且开始工作后我也没有太多时间去购物。

transcript.en: MAN: There are two things you need to know about Larch Furniture.
transcript.cn: 男士：关于拉奇家具，你需要了解两件事。

transcript.en: First of all, you have to take out insurance on the furniture, and you need to organise that yourself.
transcript.cn: 首先，你必须为家具投保，而且你需要自己组织这件事。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: That wouldn't be too hard.
transcript.cn: 谢利：那不会太难。

transcript.en: MAN: Also, you can't take out a contract for less than six months, but I fgure that might not be a problem for you.
transcript.cn: 男人：还有，你不能签订少于六个月的合同，但我猜这对你来说可能不是问题。

transcript.en: You're renting the house for twelve months after all, aren't you?
transcript.cn: 毕竟，你要把房子租出去十二个月，不是吗？

transcript.en: SHELLEY: Yes. OK, well I …
transcript.cn: 谢利：是的。好的，那么我......

transcript.en: MAN: … sorry to interrupt. I just thought of another furniture rental company.
transcript.cn: 男人：...... 抱歉打断一下。我只是想到了另一家家具租赁公司。

transcript.en: It's called Space Rentals. And it's located very near to the house.
transcript.cn: 它叫太空租赁。而且它离房子很近。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: OK.
transcript.cn: 谢利：好的。

transcript.en: MAN: I don't have any information about their charges, so it's best to use their app to find out what it would cost you to use them.
transcript.cn: 男人：我没有任何关于他们收费的信息，所以最好使用他们的应用程序了解一下使用他们的成本。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: OK thanks. I'll do that.
transcript.cn: 谢利：好的，谢谢。我会这么做的。

transcript.en: MAN: One good thing about that company is that if you don't like the furniture once it's delivered, you can request exchanges – as long as you do that within a week of receiving it.
transcript.cn: 男士：那家公司的一个好处是，如果你在收到家具后不喜欢，可以要求更换 —— 只要你在收到后的一周内这样做。

transcript.en: SHELLEY: That sounds really great. OK. Well thanks very much. That's so helpful …
transcript.cn: 谢利：这听起来真的很棒。好的。非常感谢。这真的很有帮助......

groups.title: Bidcaster Community Archaeology Project
groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Who was responsible for starting the community project?

options.title: A
options.content: the castle owners

options.title: B
options.content: a national charity

options.title: C
options.content: the local council

questions.analysis: 题目询问“谁负责发起这个社区项目”。原文中提到“NHA, a charity which sets up projects like this up and down the country”，其中“sets up”（建立、设立）与题干中的“starting”（发起）为同义替换，且明确指出NHA是“a charity”（慈善机构），因此负责发起项目的是全国性慈善机构，答案为B。

questions.title: How was the gold coin found?

options.title: A
options.content: Heavy rain had removed some of the soil.

options.title: B
options.content: The ground was dug up by wild rabbits.

options.title: C
options.content: A person with a metal detector searched the area.

questions.analysis: 题目询问金币的发现方式。答案句明确指出“一场暴风雨冲走了一些泥土和沙子之后，一个步行者在地上发现了它”。其中“rainstorm”（暴风雨）对应选项A中的“Heavy rain”（大雨），“washed away”（冲走）对应“removed”（移走），“earth and sand”（泥土和沙子）对应“soil”（土壤），三者构成完整同义替换，因此选项A正确。

questions.title: What led the archaeologists to believe there was an ancient village on this site?

options.title: A
options.content: the lucky discovery of old records

options.title: B
options.content: the bases of several structures visible in the grass

options.title: C
options.content: the unusual stones found near the castle

questions.analysis: 题目询问“是什么让考古学家相信这个遗址上有一个古村落”。听力原文中提到，在业余历史学家Peter Swift提出这里曾有村落的想法后，团队“偶然发现了一些旧地图和文件”，上面有五百年前的废墟建筑图画，随后“知道有重大发现了”。“old maps and documents”（旧地图和文件）与选项A中的“old records”（旧记录）是同义替换，这一发现直接促使考古学家相信古村落的存在，因此答案为A。

questions.title: What are the team still hoping to find?

options.title: A
options.content: everyday pottery

options.title: B
options.content: animal bones

options.title: C
options.content: pieces of jewellery

questions.analysis: 题目询问团队仍然希望找到什么。在听力原文中，通过转折词"but"引出关键信息"still waiting to uncover any such items"，其中"such items"指代的是前文提到的"brooches and other jewellery"。"still waiting to uncover"与题干中的"still hoping to find"是同义替换，表明珠宝类物品是团队尚未找到且仍在期待发现的，因此正确答案是与"jewellery"对应的选项。

questions.title: What was found on the other side of the river to the castle?

options.title: A
options.content: the remains of a large palace

options.title: B
options.content: the outline of fields

options.title: C
options.content: a number of small huts

questions.analysis: 题目询问在城堡对面的河对岸发现了什么。根据答案句，河对岸发现了“borders of an ancient field system”（古代田地系统的边界），其中“borders”（边界）与选项B中的“outline”（轮廓）为同义替换，“field system”（田地系统）对应选项B的“fields”（田地），因此选项B正确。

questions.title: What do the team plan to do after work ends this summer?

options.title: A
options.content: prepare a display for a museum

options.title: B
options.content: take part in a television programme

options.title: C
options.content: start to organise school visits

questions.analysis: 题目询问团队在今年夏天工作结束后计划做什么。根据听力原文，当提到“This summer’s work will end soon”（今年夏天的工作很快就要结束了）这一与定位词对应的信号词后，紧接着说明“In the meantime, we’re putting on a series of guided tours for school groups this autumn”（与此同时，在今年秋天，我们将为学校团体举办一系列的导览活动）。其中“putting on... guided tours”（举办导览活动）与选项C中的“organise visits”（组织参观）为同义替换，“school groups”（学校团体）对应“school”，因此答案为C。

groups.desc:
Questions 17-20
Label the map below.
Drag the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 17-20.

questions.title: bridge foundations
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文对“bridge”位置的描述，从地图起点“You are here”出发，需“take the main path ahead of you, go straight on”（沿着前面的主路直走），“till the path bends to the left”（直到路向左拐弯），此时会看到“a smaller track leading off to the right”（一条通向右边的小路），“Follow that to take you to the river”（沿着这条小路就能到河边）。观察地图，起点前方为主路，直走至主路向左拐弯处，右侧有一条小路直接通向河边的B点，与描述完全匹配，因此答案为B。

questions.title: rubbish pit
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，垃圾坑的位置被描述为“very near the castle walls in the northwest corner of the site”（非常靠近城堡墙壁，位于遗址的西北角）。结合地图，左侧明确标注“Castle walls”（城堡墙壁），且地图右下角指南针指示“上北（N）、左西（W）”，因此“西北角”对应地图左上角靠近城堡墙壁的位置，即选项A。

questions.title: meeting hall
questions.analysis: 听力原文明确提到会议厅是“遗址中心区域最大的建筑（the largest structure in the central area of the site）”且“紧邻当前挖掘区域（next to the current excavation area）”。观察地图，“Site of current dig”（当前挖掘区域）已明确标注，其旁边有D和G两个选项。通过对比两者大小，G的方框明显大于D，符合“最大的建筑”这一关键描述，因此G是正确答案。

questions.title: fish pond
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文对鱼塘（Fish pond）位置的描述：从起点"You are here"出发，在第一个信息板处右转，沿着小路进入树林，在走出树林之前，鱼塘就在右手边。地图上，从起点出发，向右（东）的路径会进入一片有树木标识的区域。在该区域内，位于路径右侧（即地图上该路径的北侧，因为行进方向大致向东）的是E点。同时，原文提到"如果到达河边就走得太远了"，E点位于树林内部，未到达河边，符合描述。因此，正确答案是E。

transcript.en: PART 2
Hello, I'm Hayden. I'm one of the archaeologists investigating the site here at Bidcaster.
transcript.cn: 你好，我是海登。我是在比德卡斯特调查这个遗址的考古学家之一。

transcript.en: This is the third summer for this community project, and most of the people digging here are volunteers.
transcript.cn: 这是这个社区项目的第三个夏天，大多数在这里发掘的人都是志愿者。

transcript.en: I'm a full-time archaeologist for the town council, but I was asked to join the project by NHA, a charity which sets up projects like this up and down the country.
transcript.cn: 我是镇议会的全职考古学家，但我被 NHA 邀请加入这个项目。NHA 是一个慈善机构，在全国各地设立类似的项目。

transcript.en: As you can see, we're next to Bidcaster Castle, which is great because the owners let us use their facilities.
transcript.cn: 如你所见，我们就在比德卡斯特城堡旁边，这个地方很棒，因为业主允许我们使用他们的设施。

transcript.en: So, how did we get to where we are today? Many archaeology projects happen when an ancient object is found, and in our case, that object was a gold coin.
transcript.cn: 那么，我们是如何走到今天这一步的呢？许多考古项目都是因为发现了古代物品而启动的，而在我们的案例中，那个物品是一枚金币。

transcript.en: Coins are often found by people using metal detectors to look for things buried in the ground – or coins are uncovered when wild animals like rabbits have been digging tunnels.
transcript.cn: 硬币通常是人们使用金属探测器寻找埋在地下的东西时发现的 —— 或者是在野生动物 (如兔子) 挖掘隧道时发现的。

transcript.en: Here, a walker found it on the ground after a rainstorm washed away some of the earth and sand.
transcript.cn: 在这里，一位步行者在一场暴雨冲走了一些土壤和沙子后，在地上发现了它。

transcript.en: When the story of the gold coin hit the news, Peter Swift, an amateur historian, contacted me to say he believed there had been a village on this site, centuries before the castle was built.
transcript.cn: 当金币的故事成为新闻时，业余历史学家彼得・斯威夫特联系我，说他相信在这个地方曾经有过一个村庄，比城堡建造的时间早了几个世纪。

transcript.en: Just by chance, the team found some old maps and documents in our library which showed five-hundred-year-old drawings of ruined buildings on the grassy area between the outer stone walls of the castle and the river.
transcript.cn: 纯属偶然，团队在我们的图书馆里发现了一些旧地图和文件，展示了城堡外石墙和河流之间草地上五百年前的废弃建筑图纸。

transcript.en: We knew then we were onto something.
transcript.cn: 那时我们就知道我们找到了一些线索。

transcript.en: Over the three summers the team has been here, we've found the remains of several buildings, and more broken pots than you can count! Normally you'd expect to find brooches and other jewellery, but we're still waiting to uncover any such items.
transcript.cn: 在团队来到这里的三个夏天里，我们发现了几座建筑的遗迹，以及数不胜数的破碎陶罐！通常你会期待找到胸针和其他珠宝，但我们仍在等待发现任何类似的物品。

transcript.en: The people who once lived here were skilled at making tools from animal bones, as you'll see when you visit the exhibition.
transcript.cn: 曾经居住在这里的人们擅长用动物骨头制作工具，你参观展览时就会看到这一点。

transcript.en: Besides the discovery of the village, we've also found evidence of human activity on the other side of the river – no other houses or huts so far, but we can see the borders of an ancient field system.
transcript.cn: 除了发现村庄外，我们还在河对岸发现了人类活动的证据 —— 目前还没有其他房屋或小屋，但我们可以看到一个古老田野系统的边缘。

transcript.en: At one point we found a long wall and thought it was an ancient palace, but it turned out to be a modern wall.
transcript.cn: 在某个地方，我们发现了一堵长墙，原以为是一座古老的宫殿，结果发现那是一堵现代化的墙。

transcript.en: This summer's work will end soon, but we'll be back next summer.
transcript.cn: 这个夏天的工作很快就会结束，但我们明年夏天还会再来。

transcript.en: In the meantime, we're putting on a series of guided tours for school groups this autumn. Oh and, maybe you saw the TV documentary about our project.
transcript.cn: 与此同时，我们将在今年秋天为学校团体开展一系列导览活动。哦，对了，也许你看过关于我们项目的电视纪录片。

transcript.en: That suggested the objects we've found are going to the town's museum, but we don't know that for sure yet.
transcript.cn: 这表明我们发现的物品可能被送往镇上的博物馆，但我们还不能确定。

transcript.en: When you enter the site, please make sure you keep to the paths at all times.
transcript.cn: 当你进入现场时，请确保始终沿着小路前进。

transcript.en: There are a few other things – the highlights of the site, if you like – that I want to mention.
transcript.cn: 还有一些其他事情 —— 如果你愿意的话，可以称之为网站的亮点 —— 我想提一下。

transcript.en: Take a look at the map; our present location is marked at the bottom.
transcript.cn: 看看地图；我们当前的位置标记在底部。

transcript.en: This year we've identified the foundations of an ancient bridge.
transcript.cn: 今年，我们发现了一座古桥的基础。

transcript.en: And it's really exciting today because a team of divers are in the river searching for lost objects.
transcript.cn: 今天真的很令人兴奋，因为一队潜水员正在河里寻找丢失的物品。

transcript.en: To reach the bridge, take the main path ahead of you, go straight on and keep going till the path bends to the left.
transcript.cn: 要到达那座桥，请沿着前方的主路一直走，直到小路向左拐。

transcript.en: You'll see a smaller track leading off to the right.
transcript.cn: 你会看到一条向右延伸的小路。

transcript.en: Follow that to take you to the river, where the divers are.
transcript.cn: 沿着那条路走就能到达河边，那里有潜水员。

transcript.en: You might be interested to see the rubbish pit.
transcript.cn: 你可能会有兴趣看看垃圾坑。

transcript.en: This is very near the castle walls in the northwest corner of the site.
transcript.cn: 这里非常靠近遗址西北角的城堡围墙。

transcript.en: It actually dates to the time of the castle and not the ancient village.
transcript.cn: 这实际上是在城堡时期而不是古村庄时期发现的。

transcript.en: We found oyster shells and fish bones, and we assume they were thrown from the castle kitchen above.
transcript.cn: 我们发现了牡蛎壳和鱼骨，我们推测这些是从上面的城堡厨房里扔下来的。

transcript.en: One area we excavated in the first summer uncovered the site of a meeting hall.
transcript.cn: 我们在第一个夏天发掘的一个区域发现了一个会议厅的遗址。

transcript.en: We knew it was an important building because it had two rows of post holes, deep enough to support a large roof.
transcript.cn: 我们知道这是一座重要的建筑，因为它有两排柱孔，深度足以支撑一个大型屋顶。

transcript.en: It's the largest structure in the central area of the site, next to the current excavation area.
transcript.cn: 这是该遗址中心区域最大的建筑，紧邻目前的发掘区域。

transcript.en: Last year we discovered a fish pond in the ancient village.
transcript.cn: 去年，我们在古老的村庄里发现了一个鱼塘。

transcript.en: Normally these were beside a river.
transcript.cn: 通常，这些都在河流旁边。

transcript.en: The pond here is further away, but it's possible the river has moved slightly.
transcript.cn: 这里的池塘离得更远，但河流可能稍微移动了一些。

transcript.en: Anyway, to get there from here, you turn right at the first information board you come to, and follow the path into the trees.
transcript.cn: 总之，要从这里到达那里，你在遇到的第一个信息指示牌处右转，然后沿着小路走进树林。

transcript.en: Before you come out of the trees, you'll see it on your right.
transcript.cn: 在你走出树林之前，你会在右边看到它。

transcript.en: If you reach the river, you've gone too far. So, does anyone …
transcript.cn: 如果你到达河边，你就已经走得太远了。那么，有人......

groups.title: Project on Theatre Programmes
groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Finn was pleased to discover that their topic

options.title: A
options.content: was not familiar to their module leader.

options.title: B
options.content: had not been chosen by other students.

options.title: C
options.content: did not prove to be difficult to research.

questions.analysis: 题目询问Finn因发现他们的主题什么而感到高兴。根据听力原文，Finn提到“I’m glad we picked this subject. No one else on the course is doing the same as us”，其中“No one else on the course is doing the same as us”表明课程中没有其他学生选择相同的主题，这与选项B“had not been chosen by other students”表述一致，因此答案为B。

questions.title: Maya says a mistaken belief about theatre programmes is that

options.title: A
options.content: theatres pay companies to produce them.

options.title: B
options.content: few theatre-goers buy them nowadays.

options.title: C
options.content: they contain far more adverts than previously.

questions.analysis: 题目询问Maya提到的关于剧院节目单的错误观念。原文中“Contrary to what many people think”明确提示这是一个普遍的错误认知，其后内容“theatres don’t hire people to do the programmes”（剧院不雇人制作节目单）直接反驳了“剧院付钱给公司制作节目单”的错误观念，因此正确答案为A。

questions.title: Finn was surprised that, in early British theatre, programmes

options.title: A
options.content: were difficult for audiences to obtain.

options.title: B
options.content: were given out free of charge.

options.title: C
options.content: were seen as a kind of contract.

questions.analysis: 题目询问Finn对早期英国剧院节目单感到惊讶的内容。听力中Finn明确提到“hadn’t realised”（对应题干“was surprised”），随后指出节目单上若列出著名演员，观众便期望该演员出演，若未出演则会被指责“breaking their agreement with the audience”（违反与观众的约定）。这里的“agreement”（约定）与选项C中的“contract”（合同）为同义替换，表明节目单被视为一种具有约束力的约定，因此C选项正确。

questions.title: Maya feels their project should include an explanation of why companies of actors

options.title: A
options.content: promoted their own plays.

options.title: B
options.content: performed plays outdoors.

options.title: C
options.content: had to tour with their plays.

questions.analysis: Maya认为项目应解释戏班子（companies of actors）的行为原因。原文提到当时普通人大多不识字，节目单宣传价值有限，因此戏班子通过在街上游行、敲鼓、宣告即将到来的演出（announcing their upcoming performances）来宣传戏剧，这一行为的核心目的是“宣传自己的戏剧”，与选项A“promoted their own plays”相符。

questions.title: Finn and Maya both think that, compared to nineteenth-century programmes, those from the eighteenth century

options.title: A
options.content: were more original.

options.title: B
options.content: were more colourful.

options.title: C
options.content: were more informative.

questions.analysis: 题目要求比较18世纪和19世纪的戏剧节目单，Finn明确指出18世纪节目单“superior because they told the theatre-goers so many things”（更好是因为它们告诉看戏的人很多信息），Maya补充说明这些信息包括“the writer, the plot, and sometimes the history of the play”（作家、剧情，有时还有戏剧的历史）。“told so many things”及具体列举的信息内容，表明18世纪节目单包含的信息更丰富，与选项C“were more informative”（更具信息性）直接对应，因此C为正确答案。

questions.title: Maya doesn't fully understand why, in the twentieth century,

options.title: A
options.content: very few theatre programmes were printed in the USA.

options.title: B
options.content: British theatre programmes failed to develop for so long.

options.title: C
options.content: theatre programmes in Britain copied fashions from the USA.

questions.analysis: 通过定位词“Maya”和“twentieth century”可以锁定对话中Maya对20世纪剧院节目单的讨论。Maya明确表示“What I don’t really get is that...”（我不太明白的是），后面接的内容就是她不理解的具体情况，即英国的节目单在战后“didn’t go back to being more than one sheet, or change in any way for over twenty-five years”（没有恢复成多页，并且在超过二十五年的时间里没有任何变化）。这与选项B“British theatre programmes failed to develop for so long”（英国剧院节目单长时间未能发展）的表述相符，“failed to develop”是对“didn’t change in any way for over twenty-five years”的同义概括。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What comment is made about the programme for each of the following shows?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

list.title: B
list.content: It is historically significant for a country.

list.title: C
list.content: It was effective at attracting audiences.

list.title: D
list.content: It is included in a recent project.

list.title: E
list.content: It contains insights into the show.

questions.title: Ruy Blas
questions.analysis: 听力原文中，当提到“Ruy Blas”的节目册时，Maya评价其“好到可以放进画框里挂在墙上，图像太美了”。“放进画框挂在墙上”是对待艺术品的典型方式，“图像太美”直接体现了其艺术性，这与选项F“resembles an artwork（像一件艺术品）”的含义完全一致，因此答案为F。

questions.title: Man of La Mancha
questions.analysis: 答案句中提到该节目单"contains articles written by members of the theatre company"（包含剧团成员写的文章），并说明"we can learn how the production was created, and the thoughts and feelings of the cast"（能了解作品创作过程和演员的想法感受）。这些内容均指向对演出的深入了解，即"insights into the show"，与选项E表述一致，因此答案为E。

questions.title: The Tragedy of Jane Shore
questions.analysis: 正确答案为B。原文提到《The Tragedy of Jane Shore》的节目单是“澳大利亚第一台印刷机上印制的现存最早的文件”，“澳大利亚”是一个国家，“最早的文件”体现了其历史重要性，因此该节目单对一个国家具有历史意义，对应选项B。

questions.title: The Sailors' Festival
questions.analysis: 文中明确提到《The Sailors' Festival》的节目单“来自大英图书馆几年前启动的节目单数字化馆藏项目”（It comes from the British Library’s digitised collection of programmes that was started a few years ago）。“digitised collection”（数字化馆藏）属于一个系统性的项目，“started a few years ago”（几年前启动）对应选项D中的“recent project”（近期项目），因此该节目单被包含在一个近期项目中，答案为D。

transcript.en: PART 3
MAYA: So Finn, I've done as much as I can for our project on theatre programmes. How's your research coming along?
transcript.cn: 玛雅：那么，芬恩，我已经尽我所能为我们的戏剧节目项目做了一些工作。你的研究进展如何？

transcript.en: FINN: OK, MAYA. I didn't know theatre programmes are called playbills in the USA till I started looking into the topic.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：好的，玛雅。在我开始研究这个主题之前，我并不知道戏剧节目在美国被称为剧单。

transcript.en: Even though I struggled to find many useful websites, I'm glad we picked this subject.
transcript.cn: 尽管我费了很大劲才找到许多有用的网站，但我很高兴我们选择了这个主题。

transcript.en: No one else on the course is doing the same as us, although it is one of the research areas of the module convenor.
transcript.cn: 课程中没有其他人在做与我们相同的事情，尽管这是模块召集者的研究领域之一。

transcript.en: MAYA: That might actually put some people off.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：这可能会让一些人反感。

transcript.en: FINN: I suppose so. Anyway, I hadn't realised there are actually companies specialising in creating theatre programmes.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：我想是的。不管怎样，我之前没有意识到实际上有专门负责制作戏剧节目的公司。

transcript.en: MAYA: Yes, they're quite common nowadays.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：是的，如今这种情况相当普遍。

transcript.en: Contrary to what many people think, theatres don't hire people to do the programmes, in fact companies buy the rights to publish programmes on the theatre's behalf – and then make their money selling advertising space within the programme booklet.
transcript.cn: 与许多人的想法相反，剧院并不雇人制作节目，事实上，公司购买了代表剧院发布节目的权利 —— 然后通过出售节目手册中的广告空间来赚钱。

transcript.en: FINN: It must be easier for theatres to do it that way.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：对剧院来说，这样做肯定更容易。

transcript.en: MAYA: Yes.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：是的。

transcript.en: FINN: I remember reading something about programmes in early British theatre.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：我记得读过一些关于英国早期戏剧节目的内容。

transcript.en: It said that the cast was always very important.
transcript.cn: 据说演员阵容始终非常重要。

transcript.en: MAYA: Yeah, audiences were very familiar with leading actors, and big names would draw huge crowds.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：是的，观众对主要演员非常熟悉，而大名人会吸引大量观众。

transcript.en: FINN: But I hadn't realised that if the programme named a famous actor, that's who the public expected to perform.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：但我当时没有意识到，如果节目提名了一位著名演员，那就是观众期待他表演的人。

transcript.en: And if that didn't happen, people accused the theatre of breaking their agreement with the audience.
transcript.cn: 如果这种情况没有发生，人们就会指责剧院违反了与观众的协议。

transcript.en: They would demand refunds, and, if they didn't get them, there were riots!
transcript.cn: 他们会要求退款，如果得不到退款，就会引发骚乱！

transcript.en: MAYA: Outrageous. That'd never happen now.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：太过分了。这种事现在绝对不会发生了。

transcript.en: FINN: No, people are too polite, even when they're disappointed if the star of the show misses a performance.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：不，人们太客气了，即使当节目明星错过一场表演时，他们也会感到失望。

transcript.en: MAYA: We should definitely include that information about early audiences in our project.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：我们绝对应该在我们的项目中包含这些关于早期观众的信息。

transcript.en: I also think it's important to mention that lots of ordinary people at that time were illiterate, so theatre programmes were of limited value in advertising plays.
transcript.cn: 我还认为值得提及的是，当时许多普通人都是文盲，因此戏剧节目在广告剧中的价值有限。

transcript.en: When a company of actors arrived in a town, they'd parade around the streets in their costumes, beating drums and announcing their upcoming performances.
transcript.cn: 当一群演员到达一个城镇时，他们会穿着戏服在街上游行，敲着鼓，宣布即将到来的演出。

transcript.en: FINN: Interesting. I couldn't imagine that happening now either.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：有意思。我也无法想象这种事现在还会发生。

transcript.en: MAYA: There's also an interesting comparison to make between eighteenth and nineteenth-century programmes.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：18 世纪和 19 世纪的节目之间也有一个有趣的比较。

transcript.en: FINN: Wasn't it in the nineteenth century that theatre programmes started to resemble programmes today?
transcript.cn: 芬恩：难道不是在 19 世纪，戏剧节目才开始像今天的节目吗？

transcript.en: MAYA: Yes. And, unlike programmes from the eighteenth century, they always used colour.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：是的。而且，与 18 世纪的节目不同，它们总是使用色彩。

transcript.en: FINN: And there was a greater variety of designs.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：而且设计的种类更多样化。

transcript.en: But personally, I think eighteenth-century programmes were superior because they told the theatre-goers so many things, including about the actors.
transcript.cn: 但就我个人而言，我认为 18 世纪的节目更优秀，因为它们向观众传达了许多信息，包括演员的情况。

transcript.en: MAYA: And about the writer, the plot, and sometimes the history of the play.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：还有关于作家、情节，有时甚至是戏剧的历史。

transcript.en: FINN: That's right… What should we say about theatre programmes in the twentieth century?
transcript.cn: 芬恩：没错...... 那么我们该如何评价 20 世纪的戏剧节目呢？

transcript.en: MAYA: I reckon the most important thing is the dramatic change they underwent during World War Two.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：我认为最重要的是它们在第二次世界大战期间经历的戏剧性变化。

transcript.en: FINN: When the government imposed restrictions on the use of paper.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：当时政府对纸张的使用施加了限制。

transcript.en: MAYA: Yeah, but that was only in the UK. In the USA, programmes – or rather, playbills – continued to be published in the same format ...
transcript.cn: 玛雅：是的，但那只是在英国。在美国，程序 —— 或者更确切地说，剧本 —— 继续以相同的格式发布......

transcript.en: FINN: … while here in the UK, programmes became merely a single sheet of paper folded to create four pages for text.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：...... 而在英国，节目单变成了一张对折的纸，形成了四页的文本。

transcript.en: MAYA: What I don't really get is that, after the war, they didn't go back to being more than one sheet, or change in any way for over twenty-five years.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：我真正不明白的是，战争结束后，它们在超过二十五年的时间里没有回到多于一页的状态，也没有发生任何变化。

transcript.en: I know there were paper shortages after the war, but only for five or ten years.
transcript.cn: 我知道战后确实出现了纸张短缺，但只持续了五到十年。

transcript.en: FINN: Strange.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：奇怪。

transcript.en: MAYA: I've got some pictures of programmes we could include on the slides for our presentation.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：我有一些节目的照片，可以放在我们演讲的幻灯片上。

transcript.en: FINN: I found a couple too, MAYA. Let's go through and see what we think.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：我也发现了几张，玛雅。让我们来看看，看看我们的想法。

transcript.en: MAYA: Erm …, this is an old one, for a play called Ruy Blas.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：嗯...... 这是一个老节目，为一部名叫《鲁伊・布拉斯》的戏剧制作的。

transcript.en: FINN: Never heard of that, but the programme looks very decorative.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：从没听说过，但这个节目看起来很有装饰性。

transcript.en: MAYA: Good enough to put in a frame on the wall. The images are just beautiful. FINN, what did you find?
transcript.cn: 玛雅：好到可以在墙上挂个相框了。画面真的很漂亮。芬恩，你发现了什么？

transcript.en: FINN: I've got some pages from a programme for Man of La Mancha.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：我找到了几页《梦幻骑士》节目单。

transcript.en: I thought this was a good programme to show, not because of the pictures, but because it contains articles written by members of the theatre company.
transcript.cn: 我认为这是一个很好的节目单，不是因为图片，而是因为它包含了剧团成员写的文章。

transcript.en: So we can learn how the production was created, and the thoughts and feelings of the cast.
transcript.cn: 这样我们就能了解这部作品是如何创作的，以及演员们的想法和感受。

transcript.en: MAYA: Good. I've got a copy of a programme that's now in a museum.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：很好。我有一份现在收藏在博物馆里的节目单副本。

transcript.en: It's for The Tragedy of Jane Shore and it's said to be the earliest surviving document to have been printed on Australia's first printing press.
transcript.cn: 这是为《简・肖尔的悲剧》准备的，据说它是澳大利亚第一台印刷机印刷的最早现存文件。

transcript.en: FINN: Fantastic.
transcript.cn: 芬恩：太棒了。

transcript.en: MAYA: Another programme to talk about is for The Sailors' Festival.
transcript.cn: 玛雅：另一个值得一提的节目是水手节。

transcript.en: It comes from the British Library's digitised collection of programmes that was started a few years ago.
transcript.cn: 它来自大英图书馆几年前开始收藏的数字化节目集。

transcript.en: It already comprises over two hundred thousand programmes, which is amazing.
transcript.cn: 它已经包含了超过二十万个节目，这真是令人惊叹。

transcript.en: FINN: Wish I'd known about it while I was doing my research!
transcript.cn: 芬恩：真希望我在做研究的时候就知道这个！

groups.title: Inclusive design
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Definition · Designing products that can be accessed by a diverse range of people without the need for any $22065$ · Not the same as universal design: that is design for everyone, including catering for people with $22066$ problems. Examples of inclusive design · $22067$ which are adjustable, avoiding back or neck problems · $22068$ in public toilets which are easier to use · To assist the elderly: - designers avoid using $22069$ in interfaces - people can make commands using a mouse, keyboard or their $22070$ Impact of non-inclusive designs · Access - Loss of independence for disabled people. · Safety - Seatbelts are especially problematic for $22071$ women. - PPE jackets are often unsuitable because of the size of women's $22072$ - PPE for female $22073$ officers dealing with emergencies is the worst. · Comfort in the workplace - The $22074$ in offices is often too low for women.

questions.analysis: 同学你看，这道题问的是“设计出能被不同人群使用的产品，而不需要任何【什么】”。我们首先在题干里找定位词，“can be accessed”（能被使用）、“a diverse range of people”（不同人群）和“without the need for any”（不需要任何）是关键。在听力原文中，我们听到了“accessible”（对应“can be accessed”），“as many different types of people as possible”（对应“a diverse range of people”），以及“without any type of”（对应“without the need for any”）。这些信号词把我们引向了答案句。在答案句中，“without any type of”后面紧跟着的名词就是“adaptation”，它的意思是“改造”或“调整”。题目问的是“不需要任何…”，而原文明确说“不需要对原始设计进行任何类型的改造”，所以“adaptation”就是我们要填的词。

这道题的答案是“cognitive”。首先，题干中的定位词“universal design”在听力原文中直接出现，帮我们锁定了关键信息句。题干说“universal design是为所有人设计的，包括满足有【 】问题的人的需求”，而原文明确提到“Universal design aims to make products that work for everyone and that includes considering the needs of people who have cognitive difficulties”。这里“design for everyone”对应原文“make products that work for everyone”，“catering for”对应“considering the needs of”，“problems”和“difficulties”是同义词，所以修饰“difficulties”的形容词“cognitive”就是正确答案，它准确描述了通用设计需要考虑的特定人群的问题类型。

这道题的答案是“desks”。首先，题干中的定位词“adjustable”在原文中以同义替换“adjusted”（“desks which can be adjusted”）出现，这是关键信号；同时，定位词“back or neck problems”在原文中直接出现，进一步锁定答案位置。原文明确提到“desks which can be adjusted”（可调节的书桌），而后面解释“这也是上班族经常遭受背部或颈部问题的原因之一”，反向说明可调节的书桌能避免这些问题，与题干“which are adjustable, avoiding back or neck problems”完全对应，所以答案是“desks”。

这道题的答案是“taps”。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“public toilets”在听力原文中找到对应信息，原文明确提到“in the public toilets of countless hotels, airports and offices”。接着，定位词后的句子“Taps that you activate by sensor require no pressing or twisting movements”就是答案句，其中“Taps”（水龙头）是公共厕所里的具体物品。题干要求填入“在公共厕所里更容易使用的东西”，而原文说明这种水龙头“不需要按压或扭转的动作”，正好对应“easier to use”，所以“taps”是正确答案。

同学你好，这道题的正确答案是“blue”。我们来看一下为什么。首先，题干中的定位词是“designers”和“interfaces”。在听力原文中，我们听到了“software designers”（对应“designers”）和“interfaces”（原词重现）。当听到这些词时，就要注意后面的内容了。原文说“software designers rarely create interfaces with this colour”，这里的“rarely create”其实就是题干中“avoid using”的同义替换，都表示“避免使用”的意思。而“this colour”指代的就是前面提到的“shades of blue”中的“blue”。所以，设计师避免在界面中使用的就是“blue”。

这道题的题干问的是“人们可以用鼠标、键盘或他们的____下达指令”，需要找到与“鼠标、键盘”并列的第三个指令方式。首先，题干中的定位词“mouse, keyboard”在原文中直接以“mouse or keyboard”复现，这是明确的信号点。听到“mouse or keyboard”后，紧接着原文用“So”引出结果，提到“voice access is now a routine way of making commands”（语音访问是下达指令的常规方式）。题目要求“ONE WORD ONLY”，所以从“voice access”中提取核心名词“voice”，填入“their voice”符合语法和语义，因此答案是“voice”。

题目问“安全带对于【 】女性来说尤其成问题”。首先，我们通过定位词“Seatbelts”在原文中找到对应内容，即“as the seatbelts worn by the dummy are not adapted to accommodate them”。接着，注意到题干中的“especially”（尤其）与原文中的“particularly”（尤其是）是同义替换，这是关键信号。原文明确提到“particularly those who are pregnant”，说明安全带尤其对“怀孕的”女性成问题，所以答案是“pregnant”。

同学你看，题目问的是“PPE夹克通常不合适是因为女性的哪个部位尺寸问题”。首先，我们通过定位词“PPE”“jackets”“women's”在原文中找相关内容。原文提到“most PPE is designed to fit men... buy jackets... designed for a small man”，这里出现了定位词“PPE”和“jackets”，接着用“The problem with this is...”解释不合适的原因，后面提到“women... have much smaller shoulders than the average man”，这里的“women”对应题干的“women's”，“shoulders”直接回答了“the size of women's...”的问题，所以答案就是“shoulders”。

这道题的答案是“police”。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“PPE”“female”“worst”“emergencies”来锁定原文相关内容。在听力中，“PPE”直接出现，“female”对应原文的“women”，“worst”也直接出现，“emergencies”对应“emergency services”。当听到“this problem was worst in the emergency services”时，说明问题在紧急服务部门最严重，而后面的信号词“particularly”（尤其是）进一步指出了具体的部门——“police”。题干问的是“female... officers dealing with emergencies”，“police officers”（警官）正是处理紧急情况的具体人员，所以“police”是正确答案。
文中先描述了夏季办公室的场景：女性裹着毯子或穿毛衣，而空调开得很大，这说明女性感到冷。接着明确提到空调的标准设定温度是21度，且是为男性设计的。由此可知，这个为男性设计的温度对女性来说是“太低”的，所以题干中“办公室的【 】对女性来说常常太低”的答案就是“temperature”。

transcript.en: PART 4
It's only relatively recently that designers have become aware of the need to be inclusive when designing products.
transcript.cn: 直到相对较近的时期，设计师才意识到在设计产品时需要具有包容性。

transcript.en: But what does that mean exactly? Well, it simply means designing products that span economic, social and cultural barriers.
transcript.cn: 但这究竟意味着什么呢？嗯，它仅仅意味着设计跨越经济、社会和文化障碍的产品。

transcript.en: It means making sure products are accessible – so that as many different types of people as possible can use them without any type of adaptation having to be made to the original design.
transcript.cn: 这意味着确保产品易于使用 —— 以便尽可能多的不同类型的人可以使用它们，而无需对原始设计进行任何类型的改动。

transcript.en: Inclusive design is often linked with universal design, although they are not quite the same thing.
transcript.cn: 包容性设计通常与通用设计联系在一起，尽管它们并不完全是同一回事。

transcript.en: Universal design aims to make products that work for everyone and that includes considering the needs of people who have cognitive difficulties, which can present quite a challenge.
transcript.cn: 通用设计旨在制造适合所有人的产品，其中包括考虑认知障碍人群的需求，这可能会带来相当大的挑战。

transcript.en: Today, examples of successful inclusive design can be seen all around us.
transcript.cn: 如今，成功的包容性设计案例随处可见。

transcript.en: In workplaces it is common to see desks which can be adjusted to suit people of different heights or for wheelchair users.
transcript.cn: 在工作场所，经常可以看到桌子可以根据不同身高的人或轮椅使用者的需求进行调整。

transcript.en: This still isn't always the case, however, and is one reason why office workers often suffer from back or neck problems.
transcript.cn: 然而，情况并非总是如此，这也是办公室工作人员经常遭受背部或颈部问题的原因之一。

transcript.en: You'll find another example in the public toilets of countless hotels, airports and offices.
transcript.cn: 你还可以在无数酒店、机场和办公室的公共厕所里找到另一个例子。

transcript.en: Taps that you activate by sensor require no pressing or twisting movements.
transcript.cn: 你通过传感器触发的按钮不需要任何按压或扭转动作。

transcript.en: These are not only more hygienic, they're also easier for people with dexterity or mobility issues.
transcript.cn: 这些设备不仅更卫生，而且对于灵活性或行动不便的人来说也更容易使用。

transcript.en: The tech industry has been criticised in the past for focusing too much on young consumers but this is changing.
transcript.cn: 科技行业过去因过度关注年轻消费者而受到批评，但这种情况正在改变。

transcript.en: Many products are now designed with the elderly in mind.
transcript.cn: 许多产品现在都是为老年人设计的。

transcript.en: For example, it's well known that vision declines with age, and that we also become worse at distinguishing between similar colours; in particular, shades of blue, which is why software designers rarely create interfaces with this colour.
transcript.cn: 例如，众所周知，视力会随着年龄的增长而下降，我们在区分相似颜色方面也会变得更差；特别是蓝色的色调，这就是为什么软件设计师很少创建与这种颜色相关的界面。

transcript.en: Motor skills also decline with age and some people have difficulty doing everyday things like picking up a cup or opening a door.
transcript.cn: 运动技能也会随着年龄的增长而下降，有些人在做日常事务时会遇到困难，比如拿杯子或开门。

transcript.en: This can also affect their ability to use a mouse or keyboard.
transcript.cn: 这也会影响他们使用鼠标或键盘的能力。

transcript.en: So voice access is now a routine way of making commands.
transcript.cn: 因此，语音访问现在已经成为一种常规的命令制定方式。

transcript.en: It's worth looking at the problems non-inclusive designs cause when not enough consideration is given to a range of users, as it can have a serious impact on people's lives.
transcript.cn: 值得关注的是，当对一系列用户考虑不足时，非包容性设计所带来的问题，因为它可能会对人们的生活产生严重影响。

transcript.en: Access is one obvious example because it has such a huge impact on disabled people's independence.
transcript.cn: 访问是一个明显的例子，因为它对残疾人的独立性有着巨大的影响。

transcript.en: Not being able to access public transport because buses or trains are not wheelchair-friendly means many disabled people can't go out unless someone goes with them.
transcript.cn: 由于公共汽车或火车对轮椅不友好而无法使用公共交通工具，这意味着许多残疾人除非有人陪同，否则无法外出。

transcript.en: Safety is another issue.
transcript.cn: 安全是另一个问题。

transcript.en: Inexplicably, most cars are still crash-tested using a dummy based on an average-sized male.
transcript.cn: 令人费解的是，大多数汽车仍然使用基于中等身材男性的假人进行碰撞测试。

transcript.en: This has safety implications for all women, particularly those who are pregnant, as the seatbelts worn by the dummy are not adapted to accommodate them.
transcript.cn: 这对所有女性，特别是孕妇来说都有安全隐患，因为假人所系的安全带无法适应她们的需求。

transcript.en: Over the past 100 years, workplaces in the UK have, on the whole, become considerably safer.
transcript.cn: 在过去的 100 年里，英国的工作场所总体上变得相当安全。

transcript.en: Employers are legally required to provide well-maintained personal protective equipment or PPE – anything from goggles to full body suits – to workers who need it, free of charge.
transcript.cn: 法律要求雇主向需要的员工免费提供维护良好的个人防护装备或 PPE—— 从护目镜到全身防护服的任何物品。

transcript.en: But most PPE is designed to fft men.
transcript.cn: 然而，大多数个人防护装备的设计目的都是为了吓唬男性。

transcript.en: A recent report found that employers often think that when it comes to female workers, all they need to do to comply with this legal requirement is to buy jackets, for example, designed for a small man.
transcript.cn: 最近的一份报告发现，雇主们经常认为，当涉及到女性员工时，他们只需要购买一件为小个子男性设计的夹克就能满足这一法律要求。

transcript.en: The problem with this is that women can be tall and still have much smaller shoulders than the average man.
transcript.cn: 这样做的问题在于，即使女性身材高挑，她们的肩膀仍然比一般男性要小得多。

transcript.en: Ill-fitting PPE such as hi-vis jackets, vests and body armour can put women at risk.
transcript.cn: 不合身的个人防护装备，如防弹夹克、背心和防弹衣，可能会使女性处于危险之中。

transcript.en: The report found that 95% of women said that their PPE often hampered their work and that this problem was worst in the emergency services, particularly the police.
transcript.cn: 报告发现，95% 的女性表示她们的个人防护装备经常妨碍她们的工作，这个问题在应急服务中最为严重，尤其是在警察部门。

transcript.en: Another problem is related to comfort at work.
transcript.cn: 另一个问题与工作舒适度有关。

transcript.en: A very common scenario in offices in summertime is to see women wrapped in blankets or wearing sweaters while the air conditioning is on high.
transcript.cn: 在夏季的办公室里，一个非常常见的场景是，当空气调节开得很高时，女性会裹着毯子或穿着毛衣。

transcript.en: Meanwhile the men are in shorts and T-shirts.
transcript.cn: 与此同时，男人们则穿着短裤和 T 恤。

transcript.en: This is due to differences in metabolic rates for men and women.
transcript.cn: 这是由于男女新陈代谢率的差异造成的。

transcript.en: There is a standard setting for air conditioning to be at a temperature of 21 degrees, designed to suit men, and in most modern offices it's not possible to turn the air conditioning up or down.
transcript.cn: 空气调节有一个标准设置，温度为 21 度，是为男性设计的，而且在大多数现代办公室里，空气调节无法调高或调低。

transcript.en: This means that many offices which mainly employ women are wasting energy by having the air conditioning set too high.
transcript.cn: 这意味着许多主要雇用女性的办公室因为空调设置得太高而浪费能源。

transcript.en: As you can see from the examples I've just mentioned, there are serious consequences for designs which don't consider the needs of all users. This is …
transcript.cn: 正如你从我刚才提到的例子中所看到的，这没有考虑到所有用户需求的设计。这是......
